facts: The owner issued a late design change affecting facade works.
The approval of revised drawings was delayed by 21 days.
The contractor did not submit an updated recovery plan after receiving the revised drawings.
claims: The contractor claimed extension of time and prolongation costs.
The owner argued that the contractor failed to mitigate the delay.

evidence_spans.text: The owner issued a late design change affecting facade works and approval of revised drawings was delayed by 21 days.
The contractor did not submit an updated recovery plan after receiving the revised drawings.
Both events affected activities on the planned critical path.

reasoning_spans.text: The tribunal accepted that the owner-side design change caused compensable delay.
The tribunal also considered the contractor's mitigation failure when allocating responsibility.

final_decision: Shared responsibility with greater owner contribution.

facts: The contractor mobilised insufficient labour during structural works.
Site diaries recorded repeated shortage of formwork crews.
No owner instruction or approval delay was found during the relevant period.
claims: The contractor claimed that weather caused the delay.
The owner denied compensability and cited poor resource organisation.

evidence_spans.text: Site diaries recorded repeated shortage of formwork crews during structural works.
No owner instruction or approval delay was found during the relevant period.
Weather records did not show abnormal rainfall above the contractual threshold.

reasoning_spans.text: The tribunal found the delay was mainly caused by contractor resource insufficiency.
The weather defence was rejected because the contractual threshold was not met.

final_decision: Contractor bears responsibility for the delay.

facts: The contractor alleged late site handover.
The available record contains the allegation but no dated handover notice.
The adjudicator found the submitted evidence insufficient to establish causation.
claims: The contractor claimed full owner responsibility.
The owner argued that the claim lacked contemporaneous evidence.

evidence_spans.text: The record contains an allegation of late site handover but no dated handover notice.
The adjudicator found the submitted evidence insufficient to establish causation.

reasoning_spans.text: The claim failed because causation and notice evidence were insufficient.

final_decision: Insufficient evidence to attribute delay responsibility to the owner.
